target.name: Theseus

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Theseus is a Seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector founded by Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, and Sacha Lévy. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies spanning Seed and Series A rounds, supplemented by a broader comp universe of 10 private firms and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

The peer-median method, the primary applicable triangulation approach, identifies a median valuation of $150M across the five selected comparables: Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed), NODA AI at $125M (Series A), Ares Industries at $150M (Series A), Vatn Systems at $300M (Series A), and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A). This peer range spans $60M to $500M, reflecting meaningful stage and maturity variation within the Defense & Security cohort.

The single-method triangulation yields a median estimate of $150M, with a symmetric range of $128M to $172M reflecting reasonable discount and premium scenarios. Key uncertainties include the limited depth of comparable transactions at the Seed stage specifically—only one pure Seed comparable (Tenna Systems) appears in the sample—and stage-to-stage valuation compression, as four of five peers have progressed to Series A. Additional pressure points include market timing in defense tech funding and relative competitive positioning within the emerging autonomy and AI-driven security subsectors.
